news.t: May home sales unexpectedly surge to highest level this year: ‘More Americans are on the move'
May Home Sales Notched Their Biggest Rise This Year
Home sales surged in May to the highest level since December
Home Sales Rose to Highest Level This Year in May
New Home Sales Fall 6% in April as Median Price Surges
The Housing Market's Latest Hurdles: Copper, Lumber, Diesel and Aluminum
Homebuilder Charts Point to Rebound. Play the Barbell Approach With These 2 Stocks.
The Housing Market Will Stay Stuck Until Next Year—but Not in These Cities
U.S. Home Builder Sentiment Improved in May But Affordability Challenges Persist
Homebuilder sentiment improves on late spring surge in demand

holdings.infoBox: ITB is an equity ETF with a total of 48 individual holdings. The top holdings are D.R. Horton stock at 16.04%, PulteGroup at 9.14%, Lennar at 7.53%, NVR, Inc. at 6.88%, and Toll Brothers at 5.38%.

overview.description: The iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF (ITB) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the DJ US Select / Home Construction index, a market-cap-weighted index of companies involved in the production and sale of materials used in home construction. ITB was launched on May 1, 2006 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: May home sales unexpectedly surge to highest level this year: ‘More Americans are on the move'
data.text: Mortgage rates, which have been easing towards the end of April, are likely to remain elevated.

data.title: US Existing-Home Sales Rise to Fastest Pace of the Year
data.text: Existing-home sales in the US accelerated to their fastest pace of the year in May, with contract closings rising to an annualized rate of 4.17 million. Mike McKee reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest.

data.title: May Home Sales Notched Their Biggest Rise This Year
data.text: Easing mortgage rates and an increase in inventory lured baxck buyers, boosting sales 3.2% for the month.

data.title: Home sales surged in May to the highest level since December
data.text: Existing sales were 3.2% higher in May than the year before. The median price of an existing home sold in May was $429,300, a 1.3% increase.

data.title: Home Sales Rose to Highest Level This Year in May
data.text: The median home price was also the highest on record for the month of May.

data.title: New Home Sales Fall 6% in April as Median Price Surges
data.text: According to the Census Bureau, new home sales were at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 622,000 in April. This represents a 6.2% decline from March's rate of 663,000 and an 11.3% drop from the pre...

data.title: The Housing Market's Latest Hurdles: Copper, Lumber, Diesel and Aluminum
data.text: The typical U.S. home has more than 400 pounds of copper—and that's a problem when prices soar.

data.title: Homebuilder Charts Point to Rebound. Play the Barbell Approach With These 2 Stocks.
data.text: Technical indicators for Toll Brothers and Lennar suggest selling pressure in homebuilder stocks may be easing after a sharp sector pullback.

data.title: The Housing Market Will Stay Stuck Until Next Year—but Not in These Cities
data.text: West Palm Beach, Chicago, and Austin are bucking the lousy housing market with stronger sales.

data.title: House passes new housing affordability bill, removing developer restrictions
data.text: 'The Big Money Show' co-hosts analyze a new housing bill passed by Congress, focusing on its impact on homebuilders and market supply.

data.title: U.S. Home Builder Sentiment Improved in May But Affordability Challenges Persist
data.text: Confidence among U.S. home builders improved in May but still remains in negative territory amid rising mortgage rates, elevated costs and economic uncertainty tied to the war in Iran.

data.title: Homebuilder sentiment improves on late spring surge in demand
data.text: Homebuilder sentiment rose 3 points in May from April. The average rate on the 30-year fixed mortgage is now 6.65%, according to Mortgage News Daily.

data.title: Inside Out: Housing Market Fears Abating?
data.text: Alex Barron believes the bottom of the housing market is here. He says the "fear factor" from the Iran war has faded at this point as the summer home selling season ramps up.

data.title: New Home Sales Rise as Median Price Hits Near 5-Year Low
data.text: According to the Census Bureau, new home sales were at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 682,000 in March. This represents a 7.4% increase from February's rate of 635,000 and a 3.3% rise from the p...

data.title: Construction on new homes jumps to a 15-month high, but the real-estate slump isn't over
data.text: Home builders ramped up construction in March as the weather warmed and housing starts hit a 15-month high, but an industry slump appears no closer to ending.

data.title: U.S. Home Price Growth Slowed in February
data.text: U.S. home-price growth slowed in February as affordability pressures shaped home buyer decisions.

data.title: U.S. Home-Builder Sentiment Slips Amid Economic Uncertainty
data.text: The NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index fell to 34 in April due to rising materials prices, elevated interest rates and increased economic uncertainty.

data.title: Home sales declined 3.6% in March, getting the crucial spring selling season off to a poor start
data.text: Sales of existing homes declined to a nine-month low, amid rising mortgage rates and economic uncertainty.

data.title: Construction Business Taking a Hit From Iran Conflict
data.text: Rising prices for fuel and materials such as aluminum push up costs just as they were stabilizing. ‘This war just trashed that.

data.title: Why home builders' stocks are getting such a big boost from the cease-fire deal with Iran
data.text: Falling interest rates and oil prices, which could put more money into the pockets of potential home buyers, provide some hope for a turnaround.

data.title: Here's an overlooked reason the housing market could soon get even worse
data.text: A Seaport analyst has turned bearish on multiple home-builder stocks, saying the outlook for job growth could be worse for the housing market over the long term than the recent jump in oil prices.

data.title: January new home sales plunged 17.6%
data.text: CNBC's Diana Olick reports on the latest housing data.

data.title: January new home sales plunge to the lowest pace since 2022
data.text: Sales of newly built homes in January were 11.3% lower than January 2025, according to the U.S. Census Bureau. The median price of a home sold in January was $400,500, a decline of 6.8% year-over-year...

data.title: Homebuilder sentiment edges higher in March
data.text: CNBC's Diana Olick breaks down the latest housing data.

data.title: U.S. Home Builder Sentiment Inches Higher But Affordability Concerns Persist
data.text: The NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index rose to 38 in March, but builders continued to express affordability concerns due to elevated costs and shortages of buildable lots and labor.
